rules:
Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Wild Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Photon Blaster
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Put 1 of your Basic Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck. If you shuffled any cards into your deck in this way, draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 2 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for an Item card and a Pokémon Tool card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Triple Laser
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Cybernetic Wheels
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon on top of their deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Put 1 of your Basic Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck. If you shuffled any cards into your deck in this way, draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 8 cards. Your turn ends.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 2 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for an Item card and a Pokémon Tool card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

rules:
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

rules:
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
flavorText: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.
No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.
They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.
Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

attacks.name: Knickknack Carrying
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

attacks.name: Adrenaline Hammer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.
It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.name: Graveyard Gamboling
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

attacks.name: Springy
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.
The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people's emotions.

attacks.name: Forced Sleep
attacks.effect: Your opponent chooses 1 of their Benched Pokémon and switches it with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

attacks.name: Electro Paws
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Pawmot's fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

attacks.name: Everyone Discharge
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Lightning Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Release Rage
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it's bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

attacks.name: Twisting Strike
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.
Clauncher's claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn't appeal to all tastes.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

flavorText: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

attacks.name: Flame Cannon
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

attacks.name: Hot Bite
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Super Spicy Breath
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

attacks.name: Furious Kicks
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

attacks.name: Apply Oil
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

rules:
Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand. Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon on top of their deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Fighting Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Heal 30 damage from each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put 1 of your Basic Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle up to 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck. If you shuffled any cards into your deck in this way, draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Once during each player's turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player searches their deck for a Pokémon, reveals it, and puts it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 8 cards. Your turn ends.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 2 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move a Basic Energy from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards you find there to your Benched Lightning Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is Colorless less.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Search your deck for an Item card and a Pokémon Tool card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

attacks.name: Touring
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider's body heat warms Cyclizar's back and lifts the Pokémon's spirit.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Green-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they're out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.

attacks.name: Family Attack
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Maushold in play.

flavorText: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.
The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

attacks.name: Maddening Scent
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heavy Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Leg Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

attacks.name: Repelling Odor
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

attacks.name: Expert Nurturer
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: In search of happy feelings—such as joy and gratitude—Indeedee bustles around, taking diligent care of people and other Pokémon.

attacks.name: Enhanced Fang
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.
No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

attacks.name: Tailspin Away
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.
Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.
They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's Seviper's archrival. To threaten those it encounters, it fans out the claws on its front paws.
The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

attacks.name: Happy Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

attacks.name: Egg Rolling
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

attacks.name: Triple Laser
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Cybernetic Wheels
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
flavorText: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.
It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

attacks.name: Continuous Spin
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Rolling Shell
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

attacks.name: Knickknack Carrying
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.
Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.
Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

attacks.name: Double-Edged Slash
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.
Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Toxic Ripper
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 6 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.
Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.effect: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

attacks.name: Doom Decree
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief..

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It sharpens its swordlike tail on hard rocks. It hides in tall grass and strikes unwary prey with venomous fangs.

attacks.name: Toxic Strike
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It's thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

attacks.name: Wild Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
flavorText: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

attacks.name: Rampaging Fang
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

attacks.effect: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Adrenaline Hammer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.
The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon's habitat.

attacks.name: Sandstorm Spray
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you discarded any Energy in this way, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

flavorText: It will expand its body as much as it can and then contract itself, blasting out sand with enough force to wash away a dump truck.
Silicobra's neck pouch, which can inflate and deflate like a balloon, gets more elastic each time Silicobra sheds its skin.
Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

attacks.name: Chomp Chomp Bite
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.effect: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.
It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

attacks.name: Avenging Knuckle
attacks.effect: If any of your Fighting Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Accelerating Stab
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Accelerating Stab.

flavorText: It's said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.
They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.name: Acu-Punch-Ture
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Kick Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

attacks.name: Rage Fist
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Dynamite Punch
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

attacks.name: Raging Punch
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

attacks.name: Monkey Beatdown
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

attacks.name: Last Respects
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn't like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

attacks.name: Underworld Stroll
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

attacks.name: Graveyard Gamboling
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

attacks.name: Eye Strength
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

attacks.name: Dash Off
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.
Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.
Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.
The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon's body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

attacks.name: Springy
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.
This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

attacks.name: Second Bite
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

attacks.name: Energy Munch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They say that flower gardens created by Florges are constantly showered with a power that can heal both body and spirit.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage, and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon uses red wavelengths of light to pour its own energy into flowers and draw forth their latent potential.
This Flabébé rides a red flower. Immediately after birth, this Pokémon begins flying around in search of a flower it likes.

attacks.name: Curse Spreading
attacks.effect: Put 8 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It can generate and release gas within its body. That's how it can control the altitude of its drift.

attacks.name: Balloon Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

attacks.name: Everlasting Darkness
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
flavorText: It feeds on the dark emotions of sadness and hatred, which make it grow steadily stronger.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: It feeds on the dark emotions of sadness and hatred, which make it grow steadily stronger.

attacks.effect: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.
The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people's emotions.

attacks.name: Pendulum Influence
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

attacks.name: Forced Sleep
attacks.effect: Your opponent chooses 1 of their Benched Pokémon and switches it with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

attacks.name: Photon Blaster
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
flavorText: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.
When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

attacks.name: Electro Paws
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Pawmot's fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.
The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

attacks.effect: Choose 2 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The jolts of electricity it launches by violently strumming the protrusions on its chest easily exceed 15,000 volts.
This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

attacks.name: Junk Hunt
attacks.effect: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

attacks.name: Everyone Discharge
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Lightning Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Because of its rubbery, electricity-resistant skin, it can store lots of electricity in its fur.
Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pulse Launcher
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Basic Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Curl Up
attacks.effect: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

flavorText: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

attacks.name: Release Rage
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it's bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

attacks.name: Sweeping Tackle
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.
It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.
It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

attacks.name: Undersea Tunnel
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

attacks.name: Dig a Little
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

attacks.name: Twisting Strike
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.
A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.
These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.
This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

attacks.name: Vivid Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

attacks.name: Aqua Cannon
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: The cannonballs of seawater that Clawitzer launches from its claw are powerful enough to punch through tanker hulls.
Clauncher's claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn't appeal to all tastes.

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.
It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

attacks.name: Tyrannical Tail
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 180 more damage.

attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

flavorText: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

attacks.name: Flame Cannon
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.
Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Skeledirge's gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.
The valve in Crocalor's flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.
Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco's head and flickers to and fro.

attacks.name: Concentrated Fire
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.
It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

attacks.name: Bright Flame

flavorText: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

attacks.name: Increasing Spice
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Apply Oil
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

attacks.name: Nutrients
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

attacks.name: String Haul
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon's natural enemy.

attacks.name: Trick Cape
attacks.effect: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.
Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.
Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.
Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the back of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.

attacks.name: Miracle Powder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born in a land where flowers bloom. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.
Spewpa doesn't live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.
This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.

attacks.name: Fresh-Picked Fruit
attacks.effect: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in tropical jungles. The bunch of fruit around its neck is delicious. The fruit grows twice a year.
Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.
It prefers harsh environments, such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.
It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

attacks.name: Superpowered Throw
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: With its herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.
